name: AWS Lambda API
what_it_does: AWS Lambda's API for deploying, invoking, and managing serverless compute functions across all AWS runtimes with tight integration into the AWS ecosystem.
use_cases: Deploying and updating Lambda function code from agent pipelines
Invoking Lambda functions programmatically as part of agent workflows
Managing function configuration, layers, and environment variables
Monitoring Lambda performance metrics and error rates via CloudWatch
Building event-driven architectures with Lambda triggers from agents
not_for: Teams not on AWS (tight AWS ecosystem coupling)
GPU compute (use SageMaker or EC2 instead)
best_when: An agent needs to deploy, invoke, or manage AWS Lambda functions as part of an AWS-native DevOps or automation workflow.
avoid_when: You're not on AWS, or your use case doesn't require Lambda's AWS ecosystem integration.

auth.notes: AWS IAM credentials (access key + secret key) with fine-grained IAM policies. SigV4 request signing for all API calls. IAM roles are the recommended auth approach for agents running in AWS. Temporary credentials via STS supported.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 1M requests/month and 400,000 GB-seconds/month permanently
pricing.notes: Lambda is extremely cost-effective for event-driven workloads. Free tier never expires. Credit card required for AWS account. Pricing scales linearly with usage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS error responses include ErrorCode and Message. SDK adds context to raw API errors. Error codes are well-documented. IAM policy errors can be cryptic without proper understanding.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Lambda invocations support ClientContext for tracking. Function deployments are idempotent by function name + version. Event source mappings use UUIDs.
known_agent_gotchas: IAM permission complexity is high — agents need correct Lambda:InvokeFunction, Lambda:CreateFunction etc. permissions
SigV4 request signing is required — not just a bearer token
Cold starts add 100ms-10s latency for infrequently used functions
Function deployment requires uploading zip to S3 first for large packages
Concurrent execution limits can throttle agent-orchestrated workloads
15-minute maximum execution timeout — long-running agent tasks need different approach
